key_insight: BatteryMat combines machine learning, force-field delithiation, and automated DFT into a three-tier screening pipeline to efficiently rank lithium-ion cathode candidates while preserving thermodynamic consistency.
engineering_relevance: The framework enables scalable discovery of promising cathode materials from very large databases without relying on DFT for every candidate, making it useful for high-throughput materials screening workflows.
method: Use ALIGNN as the first-pass surrogate to screen lithium-containing structures for average voltage.
Validate top candidates with ALIGNN-FF delithiation profiles before running automated PBE+U or optB88-vdW+U supercell DFT.
Automatically choose the exchange-correlation functional by spacegroup and recompute the Li metal reference in the same basis to remove voltage offsets.
evidence_signal: ALIGNN-FF training on 7,610 voltages achieved MAE 0.17 V and R² = 0.94, while DFT validation on four commercial cathodes matched experimental average voltage within 0.3 V and volumetric capacity within 5%.
watch_item: The ML model is only validated against the force-field delithiation protocol, so surrogate predictions still require DFT confirmation before being treated as confirmed cathodes.
paper_title: BatteryMat: a hierarchical machine-learning and DFT framework for average-voltage screening of lithium-ion cathode materials
